reforms.description: The Administration has proposed increases in Federal support of R&D, most recently in the 2015 Budget proposal of USD 135.4 billion, a nominal increase of 1.2% over 2014, along with a supplementary proposal of USD 5.3 billion for R&D related to 'Opportunity, Growth, and Security'.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased federal R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget proposal has been enacted and funding is being allocated as planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed increase in federal R&D funding represents a significant policy change aimed at boosting research and development efforts.
reforms.source_quote: The Administration has proposed increases in Federal support of R&D, most recently in the 2015 Budget proposal of USD 135.4 billion.

reforms.description: The US is taking policy actions to improve quality secondary education, including supporting state-led standards for secondary education and preparing 100,000 more STEM-qualified teachers over the next decade, based on the recommendations of the President’s Council of Advisors on Science and Technology (PCAST) 2010 report.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving STEM education is expected to enhance the quality of the workforce, thereby supporting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy actions have been publicly announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative to prepare more STEM-qualified teachers represents a significant but narrower reform in education policy.
reforms.source_quote: The US is taking policy actions to improve quality secondary education, based on the recommendations of the President’s Council of Advisors on Science and Technology.
